company_core.products: Novee provides an AI-driven penetration testing platform that uses black-box testing to detect vulnerabilities and attack paths in real-time, mimicking the strategies of actual attackers. The platform leverages purpose-trained AI models that adapt to environmental changes, continuously validating vulnerabilities and offering customized remediation strategies based on the specific technology stack.

founders.name: Gon Chalamish
founders.background:
Gon Chalamish served as the Research and Development Group Manager at FundGuard from 2024 to 2025, following a notable tenure in the Israel Defense Forces where he held roles including Deputy Commander Chief of Staff and Project Manager. He also led a research and development group within the IDF from 2019 to 2021. Gon holds a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from Reichman University.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/gon-chalamish-a30966282

founders.name: Ido Geffen
founders.background:
Ido Geffen has over 20 years of experience in cybersecurity, having held significant roles such as VP of Product at Orca Security, where he contributed to revolutionizing cloud security, and VP of Product & Customer Success at Oasis Security, a leader in non-human identity management. He also served as VP of Customer Success & Support at CyberMDX, managing multiple departments to enhance customer journeys. Ido holds a B.Sc. in Computer Science from Ben-Gurion University and an MBA in Management of Technology, Innovation & Entrepreneurship from Tel Aviv University.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/ido-geffen

agent_output.subject: Novee's black-box AI testing approach
agent_output.message:
Hey Gon,

I'm an investor and engineer focused on data and AI infra at NEA, where we've backed companies like Perplexity, Databricks, and Together AI.

I came across Novee and am really intrigued by your approach to AI-driven penetration testing—specifically the black-box methodology and the use of purpose-trained models that adapt to environmental changes. The idea of continuously validating vulnerabilities as infrastructure evolves (rather than point-in-time scans) feels like the right primitive for modern attack surface management.

I'd love to learn more about your roadmap, particularly around how your models learn attacker strategies and how you're thinking about the long-term vision for adaptive security testing at scale.

Do you have any availability in the coming weeks to chat?

Best,
Ashley Jepson
